title: Xenial Smoky Slovakian Cheese Casserole
description: A smoky and springy Slovakian dish featuring Gruyere cheese, cooked in a clay pot and served on a Talavera plate, evoking nostalgic feelings of traditional Eastern European cuisine.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large skillet, cook the diced smoked bacon over medium heat until crispy.
Add the chopped onion to the skillet and cook until softened.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for 1 minute.
In a large bowl, whisk together the eggs, sour cream, paprika, salt, and black pepper.
Stir in the shredded Gruyere cheese and cooked bacon mixture.
Transfer the mixture to a clay pot and bake for 35 minutes.
Remove the casserole from the oven and sprinkle with chopped fresh parsley if desired.
Serve the casserole hot on a Talavera plate.
